data.title: Lands' End Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 in-line, revenue of $441.9M misses by $16.79M
data.description:
• Lands' End press release (NASDAQ:LE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 in-line.
  • Revenue of $441.9M (-14.2% Y/Y) misses by $16.79M.

  • For the first quarter of fiscal 2025 the Company expects:

    • Net revenue to be between $260.0 million and $290.0 million.
    • Gross Merchandise Value expected to deliver flat-to-low single digits percentage growth.
    • Net loss to be between $9.0 million and $6.0 million and diluted loss per share to be between $0.29 and $0.19.
    • Adjusted net loss to be between $7.0 million and $4.0 million and Adjusted diluted loss per share to be between $0.22 and $0.13.
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $9.0 million to $12.0 million.

  • For fiscal 2025 the Company expects:

    • Net revenue to be between $1.33 billion and $1.45 billion.
    • Gross Merchandise Value expected to deliver mid-to-high single digits percentage growth.
    • Net income to be between $8.0 million and $20.0 million and diluted earnings per share to be between $0.25 and $0.64.
    • Adjusted net income to be between $15.0 million and $27.0 million and Adjusted diluted earnings per share to be between $0.48 and $0.86.
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $95.0 million to $107.0 million.
    • Capital expenditures of approximately $30.0 million.

data.title: M&A Snapshot: QXO to acquire Beacon Roofing Supply in $11B deal
Energizer Holdings strengthens its debt capital structure with extension of term loan and revolving credit facility
data.description:
Energizer Holdings (NYSE:ENR) announced on Thursday the successful extension of its $760 million term loan and $500 million revolving credit facility. The company said the transactions are leverage neutral and further strengthen its debt capital structure by extending maturities for both facilities at roughly the same interest rates, providing enhanced financial flexibility in the years to come.   

The new term loan matures in 2032, and bears interest at a rate equal to Secured Overnight Financing Rate plus 200 basis points per annum. The new revolving credit facility matures in 2030, and bears interest at a rate equal to Secured Overnight Finance Rate plus the applicable margin, based on leverage. The transactions extend the existing maturities of both facilities by more than four years, and the Company's weighted average maturity by over one year. 

CEO John Drabik said the latest refinancing further strengthens the company's debt capital structure and is a testament to the credit market's appreciation of Energizer's (EBR) track record of debt reduction and strong operating performance. "By extending the maturities on these facilities, we have continued to strengthen our balance sheet while maintaining a cost-efficient, flexible and prepayable debt capital structure. We will continue to evaluate opportunities to extend our debt maturities or improve our interest rate profile as we advance our debt paydown and deleveraging objectives," he highlighted. 

Shares of Energizer Holdings (NYSE:ENR) are down 13.3% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Gold Royalty misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 and beyond outlook
data.description:
• Gold Royalty press release (NYSE:GROY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.36M (+229.4% Y/Y) misses by $1.64M.
  • 1,445 GEOs for the quarter.
  • Positive operating cash flow of $1.3 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $1.2 million.

  • 2025 and Five-Year Outlook

    • "Total GEOs are currently expected to increase to 5,700-7,000 in 2025, a year when three of our seven cash flowing assets continue to ramp up towards full production. This outlook represents an increase of approximately 16% from 2024.
    • Peer-leading growth of over 360% from 2024 levels is expected in the next five years, with GEOs forecasted to increase to between 23,000 and 28,000 GEOs in 2029. The projected five-year outlook reflects continued contributions from our cornerstone producing assets, as well as new production from assets currently in development. See the below "2025 Outlook" and "Five-Year Outlook" sections.
    • Going forward, the Company's balance sheet is expected to be strengthened by greater expected cash flow generation, stable and low operating costs and our recently improved credit terms. While the Company's capital allocation priorities continue to focus on debt repayment and disciplined growth, the increased facility terms provide flexibility to pursue opportunities as they may arise."

data.title: PDD Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.76 beats by $0.04, revenue of $15.15B misses by $860M
data.description:
• PDD Holdings press release (NASDAQ:PDD): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.76 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $15.15B (+24.0% Y/Y) misses by $860M.
  • Revenues from online marketing services and others were RMB57,011.1 million (US$7,810.5 million), an increase of 17% from RMB48,675.6 million in the same quarter of 2023.
  • Revenues from transaction services were RMB53,599.0 million (US$7,343.0 million), an increase of 33% from RMB40,205.4 million in the same quarter of 2023.

data.title: H World Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• H World Group press release (NASDAQ:HTHT): Q4 revenue increased 7.8% year-over-year to RMB6.0 billion (US$825 million) in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • GAAP EPADS of $0.02.
  • A total of 11,147 hotels or 1,088,218 hotel rooms in operation as of December 31, 2024.
  • Hotel turnover increased 16.5% year-over-year to RMB23.7 billion in the fourth quarter of 2024 and increased 15.5% year-over-year for the full year of 2024.
  • For the first quarter of 2025, H World expects its revenue growth to be in the range of 0%-4%, compared to the first quarter of 2024 or in the range of 3%-7% excluding DH. H World expects its manachised and franchised revenue growth to be in the range of 18%-22%, compared to the first quarter of 2024.
  • For the full year of 2025, H World expects revenue growth to be in the range of 2%-6%, compared to the full year of 2024, or in the range of 5%-9% excluding DH. H World expects its manachised and franchised revenue growth to be in the range of 17%-21%, compared to the full year of 2024.
  • For the full year of 2025, H World expects to open around 2,300 hotels and close around 600 hotels.

data.title: Yiren Digital reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Yiren Digital press release (NYSE:YRD): Q4 total net revenue was RMB1,452.2 million (US$199.0 million), representing an increase of 14.0% from RMB1,274.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Basic and diluted income per ADS were RMB3.8 (US$0.5) and RMB3.8 (US$0.5) respectively, compared to a basic income per ADS of RMB6.5 and a diluted income per ADS of RMB6.5 in the same period of 2023.

data.title: Shoe Carnival Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.10, revenue of $262.9M misses by $13.02M
data.description:
• Shoe Carnival press release (NASDAQ:SCVL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $262.9M (-6.2% Y/Y) misses by $13.02M.
  • Fiscal 2025 Outlook
  • Net Sales: $1.15 billion to $1.23 billion, representing a range of down 4 percent to up 2 percent versus Fiscal 2024.
    • GAAP EPS: $1.60 to $2.10, inclusive of the rebanner strategy’s initial year costs.
    • Capital Expenditures: $45 to $60 million.

data.title: QXO to buy Beacon Roofing Supply for $124.35/share
data.description: QXO (NYSE:QXO) +12.2% and Beacon Roofing Supply (NASDAQ:BECN) +2% pre-market Thursday after the roofing and building products distributors announced a merger agreement in which QXO will acquire Beacon for $124.35/share in cash, valuing Beacon at ~$7.7B, or $11B including debt.
